what_it_does: Provides the Workshop UX-polish layer: three components exported on window. WSShortcutsOverlay is a keyboard-shortcuts cheat-sheet (grouped Navigate/Edit/Vi-gallery/Vi-everywhere) toggled by pressing '?'. WSOnboarding is a one-time first-run guided tour (six steps) gated by a localStorage key that explains the Vi capabilities, with dot navigation and skip/next. WSErrorBoundary is a React class error boundary that catches render errors in a subtree, logs them, and renders a recovery panel (reload or try-again) so the rest of Studio keeps working.
observations: SHORTCUTS is a static data structure of four groups; the overlay renders kbd rows from it
The '?' key (or shift+'/') toggles the overlay, ignored while focus is in an input/textarea/contentEditable; Escape closes it
ONBOARD_KEY = 'cvstudio:onboarded:engine-v2' gates the tour; ONBOARD_STEPS describes the new Vi surfaces (field pill, +Vi divider, Transform menu, chat-rail edits, templates)
WSOnboarding reads/writes localStorage in try/catch so storage failures don't break the tour; dots are clickable and keyboard-navigable
WSErrorBoundary uses getDerivedStateFromError + componentDidCatch, shows err.message in a <pre>, and offers location.reload() or this.reset()
All three are exposed as window.WSShortcutsOverlay / WSOnboarding / WSErrorBoundary
ViShape and dsa-btn classes are used (shared design-system components/styles)
standouts: An error boundary that frames the crash as 'Vi caught the crash so the rest of Studio keeps working' and reassures auto-save — recovery UX tied to the product narrative
The shortcuts and onboarding content double as living documentation of the Vi capability surface (field alternates, +Vi dividers, transforms, chat-rail edits, templates)
conventions: Content-as-data: shortcuts and onboarding steps are static arrays the components render, easy to extend
Uses the shared ViShape and dsa-btn primitives rather than bespoke styling
localStorage access wrapped in try/catch (defensive, non-fatal)
Keyboard handlers guard against firing while typing in editable fields
concerns: The onboarding/shortcuts text hardcodes capability descriptions (e.g. 'Transform button opens 7 whole-doc moves', '43 capabilities') that can drift from the actual CV_AI registry, since they are prose not a projection of it
The ONBOARD_KEY is versioned ('engine-v2'); changing the tour requires bumping the key or returning users won't see updates — a manual coupling
WSErrorBoundary catches render errors but not async/event-handler errors (standard React limitation, worth noting for an editor with heavy async Vi calls)
notes: This is the Workshop polish/safety layer: a '?' shortcuts overlay, a one-time Vi-capabilities onboarding tour, and an error boundary with a friendly recovery panel — all static-content-driven and window-exposed.
questions: Could the shortcuts/onboarding capability descriptions be generated from the CV_AI registry to avoid prose drift (e.g. the '7 transforms' / '43 capabilities' counts)?
Is bumping ONBOARD_KEY the intended mechanism for re-showing the tour after capability changes?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header lists keyboard-shortcuts overlay, first-run onboarding tour, and a graceful error boundary; the file implements exactly those three.
apparent_themes: UX polish and resilience as a distinct layer
Discoverability of the Vi capability surface (shortcuts + tour)
Graceful failure framed in the product's voice
intention_signals: Trying to make Workshop feel finished and safe — teach users the breadth of Vi's woven-in capabilities, give power users a shortcut reference, and ensure a single subtree crash never takes down the whole editor.
novelty: Modest but coherent: the error-boundary copy and onboarding both narrate Vi's omnipresence in Workshop, reinforcing the product's 'Vi is woven through everything' story at the polish layer rather than treating these as generic chrome.
contribution: Contributes the Workshop UX-polish components: the keyboard-shortcuts overlay, the first-run Vi-capabilities onboarding tour, and the React error boundary with recovery panel — the discoverability and resilience layer of the editor.
summary_for_embedding: Workshop UX-polish layer: a '?'-triggered keyboard-shortcuts overlay, a one-time localStorage-gated six-step Vi-capabilities onboarding tour, and a React error boundary that catches subtree render crashes and shows a friendly recovery panel — all static-content-driven and window-exposed.
intention_for_embedding: To make Workshop feel finished and resilient by teaching users the Vi capability surface and ensuring a single crash never takes down the whole editor.
